# BSGP7030 Assignment 3 - Linear Regression Project

This project uses notebooks and scripts written in both python and r which analyze Salary vs Years Experience.

## Overview

There is both a manual and an AI version of the project. Each will contain 2 notebooks and 2 scripts each written in python or r. This project contains html files for each notebook, a png generated by each script, an environment.yml and the setup.sh file for the environment.

The manual version, and this README, were typed completely by hand, whereas the AI version is generated by cursor AI using the composer 2.5 agent. 


## Requirements

R and python software will both be required. For this project python version 3.14 was used and r version 4.5.3. 
Python script and notebooks will require pandas, matplotlib, scikit-learn
r script and notebooks will require ggplot2

## Setup

For Manual
conda env create -f Manual/environment-1.yml
conda activate 7030_class_2

For AI
conda env create -f Ai/environment.yml
conda activate assignment3_ai

# Usage

In Manual
Running r script:
Rscript linear_regression_r.R regression_data-1.csv YearsExperience Salary

Running python script:
python linear_regression_python.py regression_data-1.csv YearsExperience Salary


In Ai
Running python script
python linear_regression_python.py regression_data-1.csv YearsExperience Salary

Running r script
Rscript linear_regression_R.r regression_data-1.csv YearsExperience Salary

# Output
R
'linear_regression_r_output.png'
Summary table printed to terminal (intercept, slope, mse, r, r_squared)

Python
'linear_regression_python_output.png'
Summary table printed to terminal (intercept, slope, mse, r, r_squared)

# Results
R_Sqaured = 0.785, years of experience explains ~78.5% of salary variation
